StrengthMultiplier:
Multiplies the HP of the lord.
Rat = 0.5
Standard = 1
Wolf = 1.5
DotColour:
The color of the dots indicating the lords strength.
Vanilla uses yellow for weak AIs and blue for strong ones.
DotCount:
The amount of dots indicating the lords strength.
Vanilla uses one dot per 0.1 of multiplier.
Type: The type of lord that spawns on the keep.
ArabArcher: Amount of ArabArchers that spawn by the keep at game start.
Slave: Amount of Slaves that spawn by the keep at game start.
Slinger: Amount of Slingers that spawn by the keep at game start.
Assassin: Amount of Assassins that spawn by the keep at game start.
HorseArcher: Amount of HorseArchers that spawn by the keep at game start.
ArabSwordsman: Amount of ArabSwordsmans that spawn by the keep at game start.
FireThrower: Amount of FireThrowers that spawn by the keep at game start.
EuropArcher: Amount of EuropArchers that spawn by the keep at game start.
Spearman: Amount of Spearmans that spawn by the keep at game start.
Maceman: Amount of Macemans that spawn by the keep at game start.
Crossbowman: Amount of Crossbowmans that spawn by the keep at game start.
Pikeman: Amount of Pikemans that spawn by the keep at game start.
Swordsman: Amount of Swordsmans that spawn by the keep at game start.
Knight: Amount of Knights that spawn by the keep at game start.
Monk: Amount of Monks that spawn by the keep at game start.
WallDecoration:
The type of decoration the lord uses when placing flg in the aiv-editor.

8 = oil pouring out with fire
9 = burning flames
10 = small flag
11 = big flag
12 = medium flag
13 = crusader flag
14 = braziers
15 = decorative skulls
21 = steam clouds
22 = disease clouds
26 = fire explosions
28 = ravens flying down
29 = medium rocks landing in water
30 = small rocks hitting structures
31 = small rocks landing in water
40 = zero gold rising note
41 = zero popularity rising note
42 = blank rising note
43 = blank rising note
CriticalPopularity:
Below this popularity, the AI sells goods aggressively to raise gold.

10000 = 100 popularity.
5000 = 50 popularity.
LowestPopularity:
Below this popularity, AI sets taxes towards TaxesMin and gives extra rations until HighestPopularity is reached.

10000 = 100 popularity.
5000 = 50 popularity.
HighestPopularity:
Above this popularity, AI increases taxes until a stable popularity modifier of up to -3 is achieved.

10000 = 100 popularity.
5000 = 50 popularity.
Farm1:
1st farm that the AI builds.

Destroyed farms will not be replaced by the same type of farm, but with the next in the queue.
Farm2:
2nd farm that the AI builds.

Destroyed farms will not be replaced by the same type of farm, but with the next in the queue.
Farm3:
3rd farm that the AI builds.

Destroyed farms will not be replaced by the same type of farm, but with the next in the queue.
Farm4:
4th farm that the AI builds.

Destroyed farms will not be replaced by the same type of farm, but with the next in the queue.
Farm5:
5th of the 5. farm that the AI builds.

Destroyed farms will not be replaced by the same type of farm, but with the next in the queue.
Farm6:
6th farm that the AI builds.

Destroyed farms will not be replaced by the same type of farm, but with the next in the queue.
Farm7:
7th farm that the AI builds.

Destroyed farms will not be replaced by the same type of farm, but with the next in the queue.
Farm8:
8th farm that the AI builds.
After ‘Farm8’ the AI will start over from the top.

Destroyed farms will not be replaced by the same type of farm, but with the next in the queue.
PopulationPerFarm:
For every this amount of people the AI can house it will build one farm.
The first farm is always built at 0 population.

Hop farms don't count towards this value and are built whenever next in queue
PopulationPerWoodcutter:
For every this amount of people the AI can house it will build one woodcutter.
When the AI reaches this amount for the first time two are built instead.
PopulationPerQuarry:
For every this amount of people the AI can house it will build one Quarry.
When the AI reaches this amount for the first time two are built instead.
PopulationPerIronmine:
For every this amount of people the AI can house it will build one Ironmine.
When the AI reaches this amount for the first time two are built instead.
PopulationPerPitchrig: For every this amount of people the AI can house it will build one Pitchrig.
MaxQuarries: Maximum amount of quarries the AI will build
MaxIronmines: Maximum amount of ironmines the AI will build
MaxWoodcutters: Maximum amount of woodcutters the AI will build
MaxPitchrigs: Maximum amount of pitchrigs the AI will build
MaxFarms:
Maximum amount of Farms the AI will build.

Hop Farms don't count towards this value.
BuildInterval:
The speed at which the AI builds.
Counts for everything, AIV buildings, non-AIV buildings and all kinds of repairs.

1 is the fastest.
ResourceRebuildDelay: The time the AI waits before trying to rebuild a non-AIV resource building.
MaxFood:
The maximum amount stored of apples, cheese, meat, bread and wheat (+ResourceVariance on top).

Amount chosen is per type, NOT combined.
MinimumApples:
If the amount of apples stored falls below this value, the AI will buy [TradeAmountFood] apples, until reaching this value again.

If = 0, the AI will never buy apples.
If = -1 the AI will sell all apples.
MinimumCheese:
If the amount of cheese stored falls below this value, the AI will buy [TradeAmountFood] cheese, until reaching this value again.

If = 0, the AI will never buy cheese.
If = -1 the AI will sell all cheese.
MinimumBread:
If the amount of bread stored falls below this value, the AI will buy [TradeAmountFood] bread, until reaching this value again.

If = 0, the AI will never buy bread.
If = -1 the AI will sell all bread.

Can be better to leave at 0 and buy wheat instead with bread-eco.
MinimumWheat:
If the amount of wheat stored falls below this value, the AI will buy [TradeAmountFood] wheat, until reaching this value again.

If = 0, the AI will never buy wheat.
If = -1 the AI will sell all wheat.
MinimumHop:
If the amount of hop stored falls below this value, the AI will buy [TradeAmountFood] hop, until reaching this value again.
If = 0, the AI will never buy hop.
If = -1 the AI will sell all hop.
TradeAmountFood:
The amount of food the AI buys at a time.
The AI only buys 1 type of food at a time.

At a low value each purchase is cheaper (more reliable to happen) but may increase the amount of purchases needed (slower restocking of goods, may cause short shortages).
TradeAmountEquipment:
The amount of Weapons the AI buys at a time.
The AI only buys 1 type of Equipment at a Time.

At a low value each purchase is cheaper (more reliable to happen) but may increase the amount of purchases needed (slower restocking of weapons, may cause short shortages).
AIRequestDelay:
The frequency with which the AI asks the player for resources.
Lower value -> more frequent.
MinimumGoodsRequiredAfterTrade:
The amount of a resource that the AI needs to have left over after a trade with the player, to agree to it.
Otherwise the AI will decline the trade.
DoubleRationsFoodThreshold: If the combined amount of apples, cheese, meat and bread is bigger than this value, the AI will give double rations.
MaxWood: The maximum amount stored of wood (+ResourceVariance on top).
MaxStone: The maximum amount stored of stone (+ResourceVariance on top).
MaxResourceOther:
The maximum amount stored of iron, pitch and flour (+ResourceVariance on top).

Amount chosen is per type, not combined.
MaxEquipment:
The maximum amount stored of weapons and armors (+ResourceVariance on top).
Amount chosen is per type, not combined.
MaxBeer: The maximum amount stored of Beer (+ResourceVariance on top).
MaxResourceVariance: The deviation allowed from any 'Max[Recource]' Value.
RecruitGoldThreshold: The minimum amount of gold the AI needs to have, before it starts recruiting units.
BlacksmithSetting:
Decides what the AIs blacksmith settings are set to.
If 'Both' is chosen, the first will be set to Swords, the second to Maces, the third to Swords, etc.
FletcherSetting:
Decides what the AIs fletcher settings are set to.
If 'Both' is chosen, the first will be set to Bows, the second to Crossbows, the third to Bows, etc.
PoleturnerSetting:
Decides what the AIs poleturner settings are set to.
If 'Both' is chosen, the first will be set to Spears, the second to Pikes, the third to Spears, etc.
SellResource01: Recource that, if obtained, is always instantly sold by the AI.
SellResource02: Recource that, if obtained, is always instantly sold by the AI.
SellResource03: Recource that, if obtained, is always instantly sold by the AI.
SellResource04: Recource that, if obtained, is always instantly sold by the AI.
SellResource05: Recource that, if obtained, is always instantly sold by the AI.
SellResource06: Recource that, if obtained, is always instantly sold by the AI.
SellResource07: Recource that, if obtained, is always instantly sold by the AI.
SellResource08: Recource that, if obtained, is always instantly sold by the AI.
SellResource09: Recource that, if obtained, is always instantly sold by the AI.
SellResource10: Recource that, if obtained, is always instantly sold by the AI.
SellResource11: Recource that, if obtained, is always instantly sold by the AI.
SellResource12: Recource that, if obtained, is always instantly sold by the AI.
SellResource13: Recource that, if obtained, is always instantly sold by the AI.
SellResource14: Recource that, if obtained, is always instantly sold by the AI.
SellResource15: Recource that, if obtained, is always instantly sold by the AI.
DefWallPatrolRallyTime:
The time that wall defense units wait until walking to their next unit spot in the AIV.
Not all wall defense units units can patrol.
DefWallPatrolGroups: How many groups of wall defense units patrol through their AIV spots.
DefSiegeEngineGoldThreshold: Amount of gold the AI needs to have before it builds AIV siege engines
DefSiegeEngineBuildDelay:
The amount of time the AI waits before rebuilding destroyed AIV siege engines.
Low value -> less time.
RecruitProbDefDefault:
The probability that the AI recruits defensive units in the default state.



Weak: almost never active, mostly when close to death.

Default: mostly the first few years of a game.

Strong: active the most.
This state is often already reached if population and unit count >40.
RecruitProbDefWeak:
The probability that the AI recruits defensive units in the weak state.



Weak: almost never active, mostly when close to death.

Default: mostly the first few years of a game.

Strong: active the most.
This state is often already reached if population and unit count >40.
RecruitProbDefStrong:
The probability that the AI recruits defensive units in the strong state.



Weak: almost never active, mostly when close to death.

Default: mostly the first few years of a game.

Strong: active the most.
This state is often already reached if population and unit count >40.
RecruitProbRaidDefault:
The probability that the AI recruits raid units in the default state.



Weak: almost never active, mostly when close to death.

Default: mostly the first few years of a game.

Strong: active the most.
This state is often already reached if population and unit count >40.
RecruitProbRaidWeak:
The probability that the AI recruits raid units in the weak state.



Weak: almost never active, mostly when close to death.

Default: mostly the first few years of a game.

Strong: active the most.
This state is often already reached if population and unit count >40.
RecruitProbRaidStrong:
The probability that the AI recruits raid units in the strong state.



Weak: almost never active, mostly when close to death.

Default: mostly the first few years of a game.

Strong: active the most.
This state is often already reached if population and unit count >40.
RecruitProbAttackDefault:
The probability that the AI recruits attack units in the default state.



Weak: almost never active, mostly when close to death.

Default: mostly the first few years of a game.

Strong: active the most.
This state is often already reached if population and unit count >40.
RecruitProbAttackWeak:
The probability that the AI recruits attack units in the weak state.



Weak: almost never active, mostly when close to death.

Default: mostly the first few years of a game.

Strong: active the most.
This state is often already reached if population and unit count >40.
RecruitProbAttackStrong:
The probability that the AI recruits attack units in the strong state.



Weak: almost never active, mostly when close to death.

Default: mostly the first few years of a game.

Strong: active the most.
This state is often already reached if population and unit count >40.
SortieUnitRangedMin:
The minimum number of ranged sortie units the AI send.



Weak: almost never active, mostly when close to death

Default: mostly the first few years of a game

Strong: active the most.
This state is often already reached if population and unit count >40
SortieUnitRanged:
Type of ranged sortie units the AI uses.
They position themself outside the castle at points where workers died.
Ranged Sorties scale up the more often they are killed. Usually around 25 units more than the given value are recruited later during the game.

Even recruited if at 0, to disable, choose an unrecruitable unit.

Sortie units always have priority to be recruited before anything else.
SortieUnitMeleeMin: The exact amount of melee sortie units the AI sends.
SortieUnitMelee:
The type of sortie units the AI sends, to intercept troops that destroy buildings.
They will return to the top of the keep when their target died

Sortie units always have priority to be recruited before anything else.
DefDiggingUnitMax:
Maximum amount of digging units the AI recruits.
Digging units have priority to be recruited before def, raid and attack units as long as the AI or an ally has moat planned.
DefDiggingUnit: Type of unit the AI uses to dig its defensive moat or the moat of an ally.
RecruitInterval:
The amount of time the AI waits before recruiting the next unit while in the default state.
1 is the fastest.



Weak: almost never active, mostly when close to death.

Default: mostly the first few years of a game.

Strong: active the most.
This state is often already reached if population and unit count >40.
RecruitIntervalWeak:
The amount of time the AI waits before recruiting the next unit while in the weak state.
1 is the fastest.



Weak: almost never active, mostly when close to death.

Default: mostly the first few years of a game.

Strong: active the most.
This state is often already reached if population and unit count >40.
RecruitIntervalStrong:
The amount of time the AI waits before recruiting the next unit while in the strong state.
1 is the fastest.



Weak: almost never active, mostly when close to death.

Default: mostly the first few years of a game.

Strong: active the most.
This state is often already reached if population and unit count >40.
DefTotal: The total amount of defensive units the AI recruits.
OuterPatrolGroupsCount: The amount of groups of defense units that patrol around the economy.
OuterPatrolGroupsMove: Whether the defensive units other than wall defenders should patrol around the economy or stand still (often by the Quarries).
OuterPatrolRallyDelay: The time that defense units other than wall defenders wait until walking to the next location.
DefWalls:
How many units of the defense unit total will be wall defenders.
They go to their AIV spots in the castle. The rest will patrol the economy
DefUnit1:
1st defensive unit that the AI tries to recruit.

If it can't afford to recruit a unit slot it tries to recruit the next one in order.
DefUnit2:
2nd defensive unit that the AI tries to recruit.

If it can't afford to recruit a unit slot it tries to recruit the next one in order.
DefUnit3:
3rd defensive unit that the AI tries to recruit.

If it can't afford to recruit a unit slot it tries to recruit the next one in order.
DefUnit4:
4th defensive unit that the AI tries to recruit.

If it can't afford to recruit a unit slot it tries to recruit the next one in order.
DefUnit5:
5th defensive unit that the AI tries to recruit.

If it can't afford to recruit a unit slot it tries to recruit the next one in order.
DefUnit6:
6th defensive unit that the AI tries to recruit.

If it can't afford to recruit a unit slot it tries to recruit the next one in order.
DefUnit7:
7th defensive unit that the AI tries to recruit.

If it can't afford to recruit a unit slot it tries to recruit the next one in order.
DefUnit8:
8th defensive unit that the AI tries to recruit.

If it can't afford to recruit a unit slot it tries to recruit the next one in order.

After ‘DefUnit8’, the AI starts over from the top of the list.
RaidUnitsBase: The amount of units the AI uses to raid the opponents buildings.
RaidUnitsRandom: The amount of units the AI may add on top of the 'RaidUnitsBase'if it feels strong and has a lot of gold
RaidUnit1:
1st raid unit that the AI tries to recruit.

If it can't afford to recruit a unit slot it tries to recruit the next one in order.
RaidUnit2:
2nd raid unit that the AI tries to recruit.

If it can't afford to recruit a unit slot it tries to recruit the next one in order.
RaidUnit3:
3rd raid unit that the AI tries to recruit.

If it can't afford to recruit a unit slot it tries to recruit the next one in order.
RaidUnit4:
4th raid unit that the AI tries to recruit.

If it can't afford to recruit a unit slot it tries to recruit the next one in order.
RaidUnit5:
5th raid unit that the AI tries to recruit.

If it can't afford to recruit a unit slot it tries to recruit the next one in order.
RaidUnit6:
6th raid unit that the AI tries to recruit.

If it can't afford to recruit a unit slot it tries to recruit the next one in order.
RaidUnit7:
7th raid unit that the AI tries to recruit.

If it can't afford to recruit a unit slot it tries to recruit the next one in order.
RaidUnit8:
8th raid unit that the AI tries to recruit.

If it can't afford to recruit a unit slot it tries to recruit the next one in order.

After ‘RaidUnit8’, the AI starts over from the top of the list.
HarassingSiegeEngine1:
1st harassing siege engine that the AI recruits.

They travel from the engineers guild to the enemy castle, shooting buildings on the way (FireBallistas also shoot units).
HarassingSiegeEngine2:
2nd harassing siege engine that the AI recruits.

They travel from the engineers guild to the enemy castle, shooting buildings on the way (FireBallistas also shoot units).
HarassingSiegeEngine3:
3rd harassing siege engine that the AI recruits.

They travel from the engineers guild to the enemy castle, shooting buildings on the way (FireBallistas also shoot units).
HarassingSiegeEngine4:
4th harassing siege engine that the AI recruits.

They travel from the engineers guild to the enemy castle, shooting buildings on the way (FireBallistas also shoot units).
HarassingSiegeEngine5:
5th harassing siege engine that the AI recruits.

They travel from the engineers guild to the enemy castle, shooting buildings on the way (FireBallistas also shoot units)..
HarassingSiegeEngine6:
6th harassing siege engine that the AI recruits.

They travel from the engineers guild to the enemy castle, shooting buildings on the way (FireBallistas also shoot units).
HarassingSiegeEngine7:
7th harassing siege engine that the AI recruits.

They travel from the engineers guild to the enemy castle, shooting buildings on the way (FireBallistas also shoot units).
HarassingSiegeEngine8:
8th harassing siege engine that the AI recruits.

They travel from the engineers guild to the enemy castle, shooting buildings on the way (FireBallistas also shoot units).

After ‘HarassingSiegeEngine8’, the AI starts over from the top of the list.
HarassingSiegeEnginesMax: Maximum amount of harassing siege engines that are on the field at the same time.
RaidRetargetDelay: Amount of time the AI waits with its raid troops before targeting a new building
AttForceBase:
Amount of attack units the AI recruits before attacking the opponent.
Increases with each attack based on what is selected in the UCP, in vanilla by 5 per attack
AttForceRandom: The amount of units the AI may add on top of the 'AttForceBase'if it feels strong and has a lot of gold.
AttForceSupportAllyThreshold: Amount of attack units that needs to be recruited before the AI executes Attack/Help commands by the Player
AttForceRallyPercentage: Amount (in%) of units that needs to gather at the rallypoint before the AI starts its attack
Unknown129:
near 0 impact. Amount(in%) of units that need to reach the rallypoint before the AI initiates a retreat, in case of their attack being deemed too weak to deal damage.

Also the amount (in%) of those retreating units that need to reach the keep before the recruitment for a new attack begins.
AttAssaultDelay: Time the AI waits with its attacking units and siege engines at the rallypoint after starting its attack before sending them to attack.
AttUnitPatrolRecommandDelay:
Time the AI waits before retreating after a successful attack.

Should normally be at 0 because the AI has no reason to wait at the opponents grave.
Unknown132:
The chance (in%) that the AI waits with an attack for an ally to attack as well.
At later stages it can also make the AI attack before the full attack is recruited if an ally attacks an enemy, to join them.
SiegeEngine1: 1st siege engine that the AI recruits for attacks.
SiegeEngine2: 2nd siege engine that the AI recruits for attacks.
SiegeEngine3: 3rd siege engine that the AI recruits for attacks.
SiegeEngine4: 4th siege engine that the AI recruits for attacks.
SiegeEngine5: 5th siege engine that the AI recruits for attacks.
SiegeEngine6: 6th siege engine that the AI recruits for attacks.
SiegeEngine7: 7th siege engine that the AI recruits for attacks.
SiegeEngine8:
8th siege engine that the AI recruits for attacks.

The can only have a maximum of eight attacking siege engines.
CowThrowInterval: Amount of stones a catapult needs to throw before throwing a Cow instead
AttMaxEngineers:
Maximum engineers the AI uses to man the siege engines in the SiegeEngine slots.
The amount should be at least as many as your siege engines combined need to be operated.
AttDiggingUnit: Unit that is used to dig up the enemy's moat during an attack.
AttDiggingUnitMax: Maximum amount of digging units that get recruited per attack.
AttUnitVanguard: Unit that runs to attack the enemy castle before every other unit.
AttUnitVanguardMax: Maximum amount of vanguard units that get recruited per attack.
AttMaxAssassins: Maximum amount of assassins who try to climb enemy gatehouses during a siege.
AttMaxLaddermen: Maximum amount of laddermen who try to put their ladder at a wall during a siege.
AttMaxTunnelers: Maximum amount of tunnelers who try to dig a tunnel in the direction of the enemy keep.
AttUnitPatrol: Units who patrol around the enemy castle and shoot enemy units.
AttUnitPatrolMax: Maximum amount of patrol units that get recruited per attack.
AttUnitPatrolGroupsCount: Amount of groups, patrol units split up into at the start of an attack
AttUnitBackup:
Units who stay at the rallypoint and only attack if there is a path to the enemy keep.
(If all other attackers die they retreat)
AttUnitBackupMax: Maximum amount of backup units that get recruited per attack.
AttUnitBackupGroupsCount: Amount of groups, backup units split up into when attacking the enemy keep.
AttUnitEngage: Units who try to attack enemy units and peasant instead of walls.
AttUnitEngageMax: Maximum amount of engage units that get recruited per attack.
AttUnitSiegeDef:
Siege defense units stay with siege engines during an attack.
May not only stay with attacking siege engines, but also harassing and defensive ones.
AttUnitSiegeDefMax: Maximum amount of siege defense units that get recruited per attack.
AttUnitSiegeDefGroupsCount: Amount of groups, siege defense units split up into during an attack.
AttUnitMain1:
1st unit that the AI recruits for the main group of attackers.
Only these units will scale infinitely.

If it can't afford to recruit a unit slot it tries to recruit the next one in order.
AttUnitMain2:
2nd unit that the AI recruits for the main group of attackers.
Only these units will scale infinitely.

If it can't afford to recruit a unit slot it tries to recruit the next one in order.
AttUnitMain3:
3rd unit that the AI recruits for the main group of attackers.
Only these units will scale infinitely.

If it can't afford to recruit a unit slot it tries to recruit the next one in order.
AttUnitMain4:
4th unit that the AI recruits for the main group of attackers.
Only these units will scale infinitely.

If it can't afford to recruit a unit slot it tries to recruit the next one in order.

After ‘AttUnitMain4’, the AI starts over from the top of the list.
AttMaxDefault:
The number of main army units that the AI CAN recruit before having to fully recruit ALL other specialist roles (AttLaddermen, AttAssassins, AttUnitPatrol etc.).
If set to 0 all specialist roles will have to be recruited before any of the AttUnitMain can be recruited.
AttMainGroupsCount: Amount of Groups, AttUnitMain units split up into during an attack.
TargetChoice:
Sets the way the AI chooses the target of an attack.

Gold: Always attacks the enemy player with the largest amount of gold.

Balanced: Attacks the enemy based on a calculated 'weakness' score. This is the most complex and nuanced targeting type.

Closest: Always attacks the closest enemy, measured from keep to keep.

Any: Attacks the next enemy in the internal player list. This can appear random but follows a fixed order.

Player: Always targets a human player first. If no human players are enemies, it defaults to Closest.
AIOxTethers_DisableInitialOxTether: 'False' means vanilla behavior, 'True' means do not build an ox tether every time a quarry is (re)built.
AIOxTethers_Logic:
Set the logic to apply.
False means vanilla, True means dynamic
AIOxTethers_MaxOxTethers: The total amount of ox tethers for this AI will never go above this value.
AIOxTethers_DynamicMaxOxTethers: The total amount of ox tethers for this AI will never go above this value multiplied by the amount of quarries.
AIOxTethers_MinimumOxTethersPerQuarry: If fewer linked ox tethers take stones from this quarry than this value, an ox tether will be built for this quarry.
AIOxTethers_MaximumOxTethersPerQuarry: If the amount of linked ox tethers for a quarry is higher than this value, no ox tether is built for this quarry.
AIOxTethers_ThresholdStoneLoad: If `stones/linked ox tethers` goes above this value for a quarry, another ox tether is built for that quarry.
RunningUnits_Macemen:
If true, maceman will always run, even when grouped with slower units.
If false they will match the speed of the slowest unit in their group (like Vanilla)
RunningUnits_Spearmen:
If true, spearman will always run, even when grouped with slower units.
If false they will match the speed of the slowest unit in their group (like Vanilla).
RunningUnits_Slingers:
If true, slingers will always run, even when grouped with slower units.
If false they will match the speed of the slowest unit in their group (like Vanilla)
RunningUnits_Slaves:
If true, slaves will always run, even when grouped with slower units.
If false they will match the speed of the slowest unit in their group (like Vanilla)
